title: Implement HTTP-like protocol from test specification across 6 files
goal:
Complete the implementation in all stub files to pass all tests in tests.py. The tests define the expected behavior.

Do NOT modify tests.py or protocol.py.

The workspace has 6 files:
- `protocol.py` — Constants and base types (READ ONLY)
- `message.py` — Request/Response parsing and serialization (STUBS — implement all methods)
- `router.py` — URL routing with path parameters (STUBS — implement all methods)
- `middleware.py` — Request/response middleware chain (STUBS — implement all methods)
- `server.py` — Server combining router + middleware + request handling (STUBS — implement all methods)
- `tests.py` — Comprehensive test suite (READ ONLY)

Each file depends on the ones before it. Read ALL tests first to understand the full protocol specification, then implement message.py, router.py, middleware.py, and server.py.

Run tests with: python3 -m pytest tests.py -v
hints: Start with message.py (parsing/serialization), then router.py, then middleware.py, then server.py. Each file depends on the ones before it. Read ALL tests first to understand the full protocol specification. Pay attention to exact error handling: ProtocolError for invalid methods/malformed requests, ValueError for duplicate routes, 404/500 responses from router. Middleware uses onion model — later-added middleware wraps earlier ones.

seed_files.protocol.py:
"""Protocol constants and base types."""

# Status codes
OK = 200
BAD_REQUEST = 400
NOT_FOUND = 404
INTERNAL_ERROR = 500
TIMEOUT = 408

# Methods
GET = "GET"
POST = "POST"
DELETE = "DELETE"

# Separators
HEADER_SEP = "\r\n"
BODY_SEP = "\r\n\r\n"
HEADER_KV_SEP = ": "


class ProtocolError(Exception):
    """Base protocol error."""
    def __init__(self, message: str, code: int = BAD_REQUEST):
        super().__init__(message)
        self.code = code

from typing import Callable, List

from protocol import *

"""Server combining router, middleware, and request handling."""
from typing import List, Optional
from message import Request, Response
from router import Router
from middleware import MiddlewareChain
from protocol import *


class Server:
    def __init__(self):
        self.router = Router()
        self.middleware = MiddlewareChain()
        self._request_log: List[dict] = []

seed_files.tests.py:
"""Comprehensive test suite for the HTTP-like protocol implementation. Do NOT modify this file. """ import pytest from protocol import * # ========================================================================== # TestRequest — 6 tests # ========================================================================== class TestRequest: def test_parse_basic_get(self): """Parse a basic GET request.""" from message import Request raw = "GET /index\r\n\r\n" req = Request.parse(raw) assert req.method == "GET" assert req.path == "/index" assert req.headers == {} assert req.body == "" def test_parse_post_with_body(self): """Parse a POST request with body.""" from message import Request raw = "POST /submit\r\nContent-Type: text/plain\r\n\r\nhello world" req = Request.parse(raw) assert req.method == "POST" assert req.path == "/submit" assert req.headers == {"Content-Type": "text/plain"} assert req.body == "hello world" def test_parse_multiple_headers(self): """Parse request with multiple headers.""" from message import Request raw = "GET /data\r\nHost: example.com\r\nAccept: application/json\r\nX-Token: abc123\r\n\r\n" req = Request.parse(raw) assert req.method == "GET" assert req.path == "/data" assert req.headers["Host"] == "example.com" assert req.headers["Accept"] == "application/json" assert req.headers["X-Token"] == "abc123" assert len(req.headers) == 3 def test_parse_invalid_method(self): """Invalid method raises ProtocolError.""" from message import Request with pytest.raises(ProtocolError): Request.parse("PATCH /update\r\n\r\n") def test_parse_malformed_no_path(self): """Malformed first line (no path) raises ProtocolError.""" from message import Request with pytest.raises(ProtocolError): Request.parse("GET\r\n\r\n") def test_serialize_round_trip(self): """Serializing and re-parsing produces equivalent request.""" from message import Request raw = "POST /api/data\r\nContent-Type: application/json\r\n\r\n{\"key\": \"value\"}" req = Request.parse(raw) serialized = req.serialize() req2 = Request.parse(serialized) assert req2.method == req.method assert req2.path == req.path assert req2.headers == req.headers assert req2.body == req.body # ========================================================================== # TestResponse — 5 tests # ========================================================================== class TestResponse: def test_parse_basic(self): """Parse a basic response.""" from message import Response raw = "200\r\n\r\n" resp = Response.parse(raw) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.headers == {} assert resp.body == "" def test_parse_with_body_and_headers(self): """Parse response with body and headers.""" from message import Response raw = "200\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n

Hello

" resp = Response.parse(raw) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] == "text/html" assert resp.body == "

Hello

" def test_parse_invalid_status(self): """Non-integer status raises ProtocolError.""" from message import Response with pytest.raises(ProtocolError): Response.parse("OK\r\n\r\n") def test_serialize_round_trip(self): """Serializing and re-parsing produces equivalent response.""" from message import Response raw = "404\r\nX-Error: not_found\r\n\r\nPage not found" resp = Response.parse(raw) serialized = resp.serialize() resp2 = Response.parse(serialized) assert resp2.status == resp.status assert resp2.headers == resp.headers assert resp2.body == resp.body def test_error_factory(self): """Response.error() creates error response with code and message body.""" from message import Response resp = Response.error(404, "Not Found") assert resp.status == 404 assert resp.body == "Not Found" # ========================================================================== # TestRoute — 7 tests # ========================================================================== class TestRoute: def test_match_static_exact(self): """Static path matches exactly.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/users") assert result == {} def test_match_different_method_returns_none(self): """Different method does not match.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("POST", "/users") assert result is None def test_match_single_param(self): """Single path parameter extracted correctly.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users/{id}", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/users/42") assert result == {"id": "42"} def test_match_multiple_params(self): """Multiple path parameters extracted correctly.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users/{uid}/posts/{pid}", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/users/5/posts/10") assert result == {"uid": "5", "pid": "10"} def test_match_wrong_segment_count(self): """Different segment count does not match.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users/{id}", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/users/42/extra") assert result is None def test_match_static_mismatch(self): """Static segment mismatch does not match.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/users/{id}", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/posts/42") assert result is None def test_match_root_path(self): """Root path matches.""" from router import Route from message import Response r = Route("GET", "/", lambda req, params: Response(200)) result = r.match("GET", "/") assert result == {} # ========================================================================== # TestRouter — 6 tests # ========================================================================== class TestRouter: def test_route_to_handler(self): """Request is routed to correct handler.""" from router import Router from message import Request, Response router = Router() router.add_route("GET", "/hello", lambda req, params: Response(200, body="world")) req = Request("GET", "/hello") resp = router.route(req) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.body == "world" def test_404_no_match(self): """No matching route returns 404.""" from router import Router from message import Request router = Router() req = Request("GET", "/nonexistent") resp = router.route(req) assert resp.status == 404 def test_handler_raises_protocol_error(self): """Handler raising ProtocolError returns error response with that code.""" from router import Router from message import Request, Response def bad_handler(req, params): raise ProtocolError("bad input", 400) router = Router() router.add_route("POST", "/fail", bad_handler) req = Request("POST", "/fail") resp = router.route(req) assert resp.status == 400 def test_handler_raises_runtime_error(self): """Handler raising generic exception returns 500.""" from router import Router from message import Request, Response def crash_handler(req, params): raise RuntimeError("oops") router = Router() router.add_route("GET", "/crash", crash_handler) req = Request("GET", "/crash") resp = router.route(req) assert resp.status == 500 def test_add_duplicate_raises(self): """Adding duplicate route (same method + pattern) raises ValueError.""" from router import Router from message import Response router = Router() router.add_route("GET", "/dup", lambda req, params: Response(200)) with pytest.raises(ValueError): router.add_route("GET", "/dup", lambda req, params: Response(200)) def test_path_params_passed_to_handler(self): """Path parameters are passed to handler.""" from router import Router from message import Request, Response captured = {} def handler(req, params): captured.update(params) return Response(200, body=params["id"]) router = Router() router.add_route("GET", "/items/{id}", handler) req = Request("GET", "/items/99") resp = router.route(req) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.body == "99" assert captured == {"id": "99"} # ========================================================================== # TestMiddlewareChain — 8 tests # ========================================================================== class TestMiddlewareChain: def test_no_middleware_calls_final(self): """With no middleware, final handler is called directly.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response chain = MiddlewareChain() req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body="final")) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.body == "final" def test_middleware_modifies_request(self): """Middleware can modify the request before passing to next.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def add_header(req, next_handler): req.headers["X-Added"] = "yes" return next_handler(req) chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("add_header", add_header) req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body=r.headers.get("X-Added", "no"))) assert resp.body == "yes" def test_middleware_modifies_response(self): """Middleware can modify the response after calling next.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def add_resp_header(req, next_handler): resp = next_handler(req) resp.headers["X-Processed"] = "true" return resp chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("add_resp_header", add_resp_header) req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200)) assert resp.headers["X-Processed"] == "true" def test_middleware_execution_order(self): """Middleware executes in onion order: last-added wraps first-added.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response order = [] def mw_a(req, next_handler): order.append("a_before") resp = next_handler(req) order.append("a_after") return resp def mw_b(req, next_handler): order.append("b_before") resp = next_handler(req) order.append("b_after") return resp chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("a", mw_a) chain.add("b", mw_b) req = Request("GET", "/test") chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200)) # b wraps a: b_before -> a_before -> handler -> a_after -> b_after assert order == ["b_before", "a_before", "a_after", "b_after"] def test_middleware_short_circuit(self): """Middleware can short-circuit by not calling next.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def auth_check(req, next_handler): if req.headers.get("Authorization") is None: return Response(401, body="Unauthorized") return next_handler(req) chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("auth", auth_check) req = Request("GET", "/secret") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body="secret data")) assert resp.status == 401 assert resp.body == "Unauthorized" def test_remove_middleware(self): """Remove middleware by name.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def blocker(req, next_handler): return Response(403, body="blocked") chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("blocker", blocker) assert chain.remove("blocker") is True assert chain.remove("nonexistent") is False req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body="ok")) assert resp.status == 200 assert resp.body == "ok" def test_clear_middleware(self): """Clear removes all middleware.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def blocker(req, next_handler): return Response(403, body="blocked") chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("a", blocker) chain.add("b", blocker) chain.clear() req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body="ok")) assert resp.status == 200 def test_chained_request_modification(self): """Later middleware sees modifications from earlier middleware.""" from middleware import MiddlewareChain from message import Request, Response def mw_first(req, next_handler): req.headers["X-Step"] = "1" return next_handler(req) def mw_second(req, next_handler): # mw_second is added after mw_first, so it wraps mw_first # mw_second runs BEFORE mw_first in the chain req.headers["X-Outer"] = "yes" return next_handler(req) chain = MiddlewareChain() chain.add("first", mw_first) chain.add("second", mw_second) req = Request("GET", "/test") resp = chain.execute(req, lambda r: Response(200, body=r.headers.get("X-Step", "missing"))) # second runs first (outer), then first runs (inner), sets X-Step, then handler sees it assert resp.body == "1" # ========================================================================== # TestServer — 8 tests # ========================================================================== class TestServer: def test_handle_valid_request(self): """Server handles a valid request and returns serialized response.""" from server import Server from message import Response srv = Server() srv.router.add_route("GET", "/ping", lambda req, params: Response(200, body="pong")) raw_resp = srv.handle("GET /ping\r\n\r\n") assert "200" in raw_resp assert "pong" in raw_resp def test_handle_bad_request(self): """Unparseable request returns 400.""" from server import Server srv = Server() raw_resp = srv.handle("INVALID") assert "400" in raw_resp def test_handle_not_found(self): """Request to unregistered route returns 404.""" from server import Server srv = Server() raw_resp = srv.handle("GET /missing\r\n\r\n") assert "404" in raw_resp def test_request_logging(self): """Server logs each request with method, path, and status.""" from server import Server from message import Response srv = Server() srv.router.add_route("GET", "/log-test", lambda req, params: Response(200)) srv.handle("GET /log-test\r\n\r\n") log = srv.get_log() assert len(log) == 1 assert log[0]["method"] == "GET" assert log[0]["path"] == "/log-test" assert log[0]["status"] == 200 def test_middleware_integration(self): """Server runs middleware around route handling.""" from server import Server from message import Response def add_header_mw(req, next_handler): resp = next_handler(req) resp.headers["X-Server"] = "test" return resp srv = Server() srv.middleware.add("add_header", add_header_mw) srv.router.add_route("GET", "/mw", lambda req, params: Response(200, body="ok")) raw_resp = srv.handle("GET /mw\r\n\r\n") assert "200" in raw_resp assert "ok" in raw_resp def test_clear_log(self): """Clearing the log empties it.""" from server import Server from message import Response srv = Server() srv.router.add_route("GET", "/a", lambda req, params: Response(200)) srv.handle("GET /a\r\n\r\n") assert len(srv.get_log()) == 1 srv.clear_log() assert len(srv.get_log()) == 0 def test_full_round_trip(self): """Full pipeline: register route + middleware + handle request.""" from server import Server from message import Response def logging_mw(req, next_handler): req.headers["X-Logged"] = "true" return next_handler(req) srv = Server() srv.middleware.add("logger", logging_mw) srv.router.add_route("POST", "/items", lambda req, params: Response(201, body="created:" + req.body)) raw_resp = srv.handle("POST /items\r\n\r\nmy-item") assert "201" in raw_resp assert "created:my-item" in raw_resp log = srv.get_log() assert log[0]["method"] == "POST" assert log[0]["path"] == "/items" assert log[0]["status"] == 201 def test_handle_post_with_body(self): """POST request body is available to handler.""" from server import Server from message import Response srv = Server() srv.router.add_route("POST", "/echo", lambda req, params: Response(200, body="echo:" + req.body)) raw_resp = srv.handle("POST /echo\r\nContent-Type: text/plain\r\n\r\nhello") assert "200" in raw_resp assert "echo:hello" in raw_resp

ground_truth:
Implementation details for each file:

(1) message.py — Request.parse(): Split on BODY_SEP to get head+body. Split head on HEADER_SEP. First line split on space to get method+path. Validate method in (GET,POST,DELETE). Parse remaining lines as headers (split on HEADER_KV_SEP). Request.serialize(): Join method+path as first line, add headers, add BODY_SEP, add body. Response.parse(): Similar — first line is status code (int()), then headers, then body. Response.serialize(): status code first line, then headers, BODY_SEP, body. Response.error(): Return Response(code, body=message).

(2) router.py — Route.match(): Check method matches. Split pattern and path on '/'. Compare segments — if pattern segment is {param}, extract value; if static, must match exactly. Segment counts must match. Router.add_route(): Check for duplicates by method+pattern. Router.route(): Iterate routes, call match(), call handler with params. Catch ProtocolError (return error with code), catch Exception (return 500).

(3) middleware.py — MiddlewareChain.add(): Append (name, func). execute(): Build chain from innermost (first added) to outermost (last added). Last-added middleware wraps earlier ones. remove(): Filter out by name, return True if found. clear(): Empty the list.

(4) server.py — Server.handle(): Try Request.parse(), catch ProtocolError -> return error response. Run middleware.execute(request, router.route). Log {method, path, status}. Return response.serialize().

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
cd "$WORKDIR"

if ! diff -q tests.py tests.py.orig > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "FAIL: tests.py was modified"
    echo "Score: 0/40"
    exit 1
fi

if ! diff -q protocol.py protocol.py.orig > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "FAIL: protocol.py was modified"
    echo "Score: 0/40"
    exit 1
fi

# Run pytest and capture output
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests.py -v --tb=short 2>&1)
echo "$OUTPUT"

# Count passed tests
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '(\d+) passed' | grep -oP '\d+' || echo 0)
if [ -z "$PASSED" ]; then PASSED=0; fi

# Count failed tests
FAILED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '(\d+) failed' | grep -oP '\d+' || echo 0)
if [ -z "$FAILED" ]; then FAILED=0; fi

# Count errors
ERRORS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP '(\d+) error' | grep -oP '\d+' || echo 0)
if [ -z "$ERRORS" ]; then ERRORS=0; fi

TOTAL=40
echo ""
echo "Tests passed: $PASSED / $TOTAL"
echo "Tests failed: $FAILED"
echo "Errors: $ERRORS"
echo "Score: $PASSED/$TOTAL"

if [ "$PASSED" -eq "$TOTAL" ] && [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ] && [ "$ERRORS" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "ALL $TOTAL TESTS PASSED"
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
